description: After 2 consecutive STT failures on same beat, surface text-input fallback. Beat scores 0 stars but session continues. Target: ≥80% session completion rate when STT fails 2+ times.

attempts.ollama_prompt:
Create or modify the following files to implement STT fallback behavior after 2 consecutive failures on the same beat:

1. **`main.js`**:
   - Add logic to track consecutive STT failures per beat.
   - When 2 failures occur consecutively on the same beat, trigger text-input fallback.
   - Update session state to reflect fallback usage (e.g., `beat.fallbackUsed = true`).
   - Ensure session continues with 0-star score for the failed beat.
   - Preserve existing STT flow and state management.

2. **`scenes/ja/metro_station.js`**:
   - Add a new `fallbackText` field to relevant `beat` objects where STT is used.
   - Example: `fallbackText: "Please say 'I want to go to Shinjuku' in Japanese."`

3. **`scenes/ja/market_stall.js`**:
   - Same as above — add `fallbackText` to applicable beats.

4. **`scenes/ja/taxi_uber.js`**:
   - Same as above — add `fallbackText` to applicable beats.

5. **`scenes/ja/hotel_checkin.js`**:
   - Same as above — add `fallbackText` to applicable beats.

Ensure all changes maintain the existing architecture decisions (hiragana + kanji display, romaji/hiragana STT input, unicode storage). Do not modify scene structure or content beyond adding fallback text fields. Do not generate new scenes or alter STT infrastructure unless explicitly approved in task lang_007.

```javascript
// main.js
const STORAGE_KEY = 'lang_003_session_v1';
const STT_FAILURE_THRESHOLD = 2;  // trigger fallback after 2 consecutive failures

const TIERS = Object.freeze({
  BEGINNER: 'beginner',
  LEARNING: 'learning',
  MASTERED: 'mastered',
});

const TIER_THRESHOLDS = Object.freeze({
  [TIERS.MASTERED]: 0.9,
  [TIERS.LEARNING]: 0.5,
  [TIERS.BEGINNER]: 0.0,
});

const SCAFFOLD_REQUEST_TARGET = 0.25;

function defaultState() {
  return {
    schemaVersion: 1,
    tier: TIERS.BEGINNER,
    currentSceneId: null,
    currentBeatIndex: 0,
    starsByBeat: {},
    starsByBeatWithoutScaffold: {},
    scaffoldUsedByBeat: {},
    beatsCompleted: [],
    totalBeats: 0,
    fluencyScore: 0,
    scaffoldRequestCount: 0,
    scaffoldOpportunityCount: 0,
    scaffoldEvents: [],
    // NEW: STT failure tracking for lang_007
    sttFailureCountByBeat: {},       // beatId -> total STT failure count
    consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat: {}, // beatId -> consecutive failure count (resets on success)
    fallbackTriggeredByBeat: {},     // beatId -> bool (fallback surfaced for this beat)
    fallbackUsedByBeat: {},          // beatId -> bool (user actually used text input)
    fallbackActivations: 0,          // aggregate counter for the session
  };
}
```

For the helper functions:

```javascript
/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *
 *  STT failure tracking (lang_007)
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------ */

function recordSttFailure(state, beatId) {
  if (!beatId) throw new Error('recordSttFailure: beatId required');
  if (!state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat) state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat = {};
  if (!state.sttFailureCountByBeat) state.sttFailureCountByBeat = {};
  
  state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId] = (state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId] | 0) + 1;
  state.sttFailureCountByBeat[beatId] = (state.sttFailureCountByBeat[beatId] | 0) + 1;
  saveSession(state);
  
  return {
    consecutiveFailures: state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId],
    shouldTriggerFallback: state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId] >= STT_FAILURE_THRESHOLD,
  };
}

function recordSttSuccess(state, beatId) {
  if (!beatId) return;
  if (!state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat) state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat = {};
  // Reset consecutive counter on any success
  if (state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId] > 0) {
    state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId] = 0;
    saveSession(state);
  }
}

function shouldTriggerFallback(state, beatId) {
  if (!state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat) return false;
  return (state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId] | 0) >= STT_FAILURE_THRESHOLD;
}

function markFallbackTriggered(state, beatId) {
  if (!beatId) return;
  if (!state.fallbackTriggeredByBeat) state.fallbackTriggeredByBeat = {};
  if (!state.fallbackTriggeredByBeat[beatId]) {
    state.fallbackTriggeredByBeat[beatId] = true;
    if (!state.fallbackActivations) state.fallbackActivations = 0;
    state.fallbackActivations += 1;
    saveSession(state);
  }
}

function markFallbackUsed(state, beatId) {
  if (!beatId) return;
  if (!state.fallbackUsedByBeat) state.fallbackUsedByBeat = {};
  state.fallbackUsedByBeat[beatId] = true;
  saveSession(state);
}

function getSttFailureState(state, beatId) {
  return {
    consecutiveFailures: (state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat && state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId]) | 0,
    totalFailures: (state.sttFailureCountByBeat && state.sttFailureCountByBeat[beatId]) | 0,
    fallbackTriggered: !!(state.fallbackTriggeredByBeat && state.fallbackTriggeredByBeat[beatId]),
    fallbackUsed: !!(state.fallbackUsedByBeat && state.fallbackUsedByBeat[beatId]),
  };
}
```

attempts.output_content:
<<<FILE: main.js>>>
/**
 * Main entry point for lang_003.
 * Handles state machine, tier progression, scaffold locking,
 * and persistence of stars + fluency_score.
 *
 * Fluency score = (stars earned without scaffold) / (total beats)
 *
 * At mastered tier, scaffolds are default-OFF (locked).
 * Target: scaffold request rate < 25% at mastered tier.
 *
 * STT fallback (lang_007):
 * - Tracks consecutive STT failures per beat.
 * - After 2 consecutive failures on the same beat, surfaces text-input
 *   fallback so the learner can keep the scene moving.
 * - A failed beat scores 0 stars but the session continues.
 * - Target: ≥80% session completion rate when STT fails 2+ times.
 */

const STORAGE_KEY = 'lang_003_session_v1';
const SCAFFOLD_REQUEST_TARGET = 0.25; // < 25% at mastered tier
const STT_FAILURE_THRESHOLD = 2;     // trigger fallback after N consecutive STT failures

const TIERS = Object.freeze({
  BEGINNER: 'beginner',
  LEARNING: 'learning',
  MASTERED: 'mastered',
});

const TIER_THRESHOLDS = Object.freeze({
  [TIERS.MASTERED]: 0.9,
  [TIERS.LEARNING]: 0.5,
  [TIERS.BEGINNER]: 0.0,
});

function defaultState() {
  return {
    schemaVersion: 1,
    tier: TIERS.BEGINNER,
    currentSceneId: null,
    currentBeatIndex: 0,

    // Per-beat star tracking
    starsByBeat: {},                       // beatId -> stars (0..3)
    starsByBeatWithoutScaffold: {},        // beatId -> stars earned without scaffold
    scaffoldUsedByBeat: {},                // beatId -> bool (highest-stars run)
    beatsCompleted: [],                    // ordered list of beatIds

    // Aggregate fluency
    totalBeats: 0,
    fluencyScore: 0,

    // Scaffold telemetry (used to measure the < 25% target)
    scaffoldRequestCount: 0,
    scaffoldOpportunityCount: 0,

    // Audit log of scaffold events at mastered tier
    scaffoldEvents: [],

    // STT failure tracking (lang_007)
    sttFailureCountByBeat: {},             // beatId -> total STT failures (lifetime)
    consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat: {},      // beatId -> consecutive failures (resets on success)
    fallbackTriggeredByBeat: {},           // beatId -> bool (fallback surfaced for this beat)
    fallbackUsedByBeat: {},                // beatId -> bool (user actually used the text input)
    fallbackActivations: 0,                // aggregate counter across the session
  };
}

function loadSession() {
  try {
    const raw = localStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY);
    if (!raw) return defaultState();
    const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
    if (!parsed || parsed.schemaVersion !== 1) return defaultState();
    return Object.assign(defaultState(), parsed);
  } catch (err) {
    console.warn('[lang_003] Failed to load session, resetting.', err);
    return defaultState();
  }
}

function saveSession(state) {
  try {
    state.schemaVersion = 1;
    localStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(state));
  } catch (err) {
    console.warn('[lang_003] Failed to save session.', err);
  }
}

function resetSession() {
  try {
    localStorage.removeItem(STORAGE_KEY);
  } catch (_) {}
  return defaultState();
}

/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *
 *  Fluency + tier logic
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------ */

function calculateFluencyScore(state) {
  const total = state.totalBeats | 0;
  if (total <= 0) return 0;
  let starsNoScaffold = 0;
  for (const beatId of Object.keys(state.starsByBeatWithoutScaffold)) {
    starsNoScaffold += state.starsByBeatWithoutScaffold[beatId] | 0;
  }
  // Clamp to [0, total] in case of stale localStorage data.
  if (starsNoScaffold < 0) starsNoScaffold = 0;
  if (starsNoScaffold > total) starsNoScaffold = total;
  return starsNoScaffold / total;
}

function calculateTier(state) {
  const fluency = calculateFluencyScore(state);
  if (fluency >= TIER_THRESHOLDS[TIERS.MASTERED]) return TIERS.MASTERED;
  if (fluency >= TIER_THRESHOLDS[TIERS.LEARNING]) return TIERS.LEARNING;
  return TIERS.BEGINNER;
}

function isScaffoldLocked(state) {
  return calculateTier(state) === TIERS.MASTERED;
}

function shouldShowScaffoldByDefault(state) {
  // Lock scaffolds default-off at mastered tier.
  return calculateTier(state) !== TIERS.MASTERED;
}

function getScaffoldRequestRate(state) {
  const opps = state.scaffoldOpportunityCount | 0;
  if (opps <= 0) return 0;
  return (state.scaffoldRequestCount | 0) / opps;
}

function meetsScaffoldRequestTarget(state) {
  return getScaffoldRequestRate(state) < SCAFFOLD_REQUEST_TARGET;
}

/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *
 *  STT failure tracking + text-input fallback (lang_007)
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------ */

function recordSttFailure(state, beatId) {
  if (!beatId) throw new Error('recordSttFailure: beatId required');
  if (!state.sttFailureCountByBeat) state.sttFailureCountByBeat = {};
  if (!state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat) state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat = {};

  const total = (state.sttFailureCountByBeat[beatId] | 0) + 1;
  const consecutive = (state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId] | 0) + 1;
  state.sttFailureCountByBeat[beatId] = total;
  state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId] = consecutive;
  saveSession(state);

  return {
    totalFailures: total,
    consecutiveFailures: consecutive,
    shouldTriggerFallback: consecutive >= STT_FAILURE_THRESHOLD,
  };
}

function recordSttSuccess(state, beatId) {
  if (!beatId) return null;
  if (!state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat) state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat = {};
  const prev = state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId] | 0;
  if (prev > 0) {
    state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId] = 0;
    saveSession(state);
  }
  return { consecutiveFailures: 0 };
}

function shouldTriggerFallback(state, beatId) {
  if (!state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat) return false;
  return (state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId] | 0) >= STT_FAILURE_THRESHOLD;
}

function markFallbackTriggered(state, beatId) {
  if (!beatId) return;
  if (!state.fallbackTriggeredByBeat) state.fallbackTriggeredByBeat = {};
  if (!state.fallbackActivations) state.fallbackActivations = 0;
  if (!state.fallbackTriggeredByBeat[beatId]) {
    state.fallbackTriggeredByBeat[beatId] = true;
    state.fallbackActivations += 1;
    saveSession(state);
  }
}

function markFallbackUsed(state, beatId) {
  if (!beatId) return;
  if (!state.fallbackUsedByBeat) state.fallbackUsedByBeat = {};
  state.fallbackUsedByBeat[beatId] = true;
  saveSession(state);
}

function getSttFailureState(state, beatId) {
  return {
    consecutiveFailures: ((state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat || {})[beatId] | 0),
    totalFailures: ((state.sttFailureCountByBeat || {})[beatId] | 0),
    fallbackTriggered: !!(state.fallbackTriggeredByBeat || {})[beatId],
    fallbackUsed: !!(state.fallbackUsedByBeat || {})[beatId],
    threshold: STT_FAILURE_THRESHOLD,
  };
}

/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *
 *  Beat result recording
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------ */

function recordBeatResult(state, beatId, stars, scaffoldUsed, maxStars = 3, opts = {}) {
  if (!beatId) throw new Error('recordBeatResult: beatId required');
  stars = Math.max(0, Math.min(maxStars, stars | 0));
  scaffoldUsed = !!scaffoldUsed;
  const fallbackUsed = !!opts.fallbackUsed;

  // Always update the raw stars.
  const prevStars = state.starsByBeat[beatId] | 0;
  if (stars > prevStars) state.starsByBeat[beatId] = stars;

  // Record the scaffold-use flag for the best run on this beat.
  // If the user re-attempts and earns more stars without scaffold, the
  // flag flips to false and the no-scaffold star count is updated.
  const prevNoScaffoldStars = state.starsByBeatWithoutScaffold[beatId] | 0;
  if (!scaffoldUsed && stars > prevNoScaffoldStars) {
    state.starsByBeatWithoutScaffold[beatId] = stars;
    state.scaffoldUsedByBeat[beatId] = false;
  } else if (scaffoldUsed && prevNoScaffoldStars === 0) {
    state.scaffoldUsedByBeat[beatId] = true;
  }

  if (!state.beatsCompleted.includes(beatId)) {
    state.beatsCompleted.push(beatId);
  }

  // Update aggregate counters.
  state.totalBeats = state.beatsCompleted.length;
  state.fluencyScore = calculateFluencyScore(state);
  const newTier = calculateTier(state);
  const tierChanged = newTier !== state.tier;
  state.tier = newTier;

  // Telemetry: only meaningful once we've reached mastered tier,
  // but we still count the run so historical data is preserved.
  state.scaffoldOpportunityCount = (state.scaffoldOpportunityCount | 0) + 1;
  if (scaffoldUsed) {
    state.scaffoldRequestCount = (state.scaffoldRequestCount | 0) + 1;
  }
  if (scaffoldUsed) {
    state.scaffoldEvents.push({
      beatId,
      tier: newTier,
      stars,
      at: Date.now(),
    });
  }

  // STT fallback bookkeeping. A successful recording resets the
  // consecutive failure counter for the beat so the learner can go
  // back to STT mode for the next beat. A 0-star fallback result
  // marks the beat as fallback-used for telemetry.
  if (fallbackUsed) {
    if (!state.fallbackUsedByBeat) state.fallbackUsedByBeat = {};
    state.fallbackUsedByBeat[beatId] = true;
  }
  if (stars > 0) {
    // Any non-zero result is a "success" from the STT layer's perspective.
    if (!state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat) state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat = {};
    state.consecutiveSttFailuresByBeat[beatId] = 0;
  }

  saveSession(state);

  return {
    tier: newTier,
    tierChanged,
    fluencyScore: state.fluencyScore,
    scaffoldRequestRate: getScaffoldRequestRate(state),
    scaffoldLocked: isScaffoldLocked(state),
  };
}

/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ *
 *  Public API
 * ------------------------------------------------------------------ */

const Lang003 = {
  TIERS,
  TIER_THRESHOLDS,
  SCAFFOLD_REQUEST_TARGET,
  STT_FAILURE_THRESHOLD,

  defaultState,
  loadSession,
  saveSession,
  resetSession,

  calculateFluencyScore,
  calculateTier,
  isScaffoldLocked,
  shouldShowScaffoldByDefault,
  getScaffoldRequestRate,
  meetsScaffoldRequestTarget,

  recordSttFailure,
  recordSttSuccess,
  shouldTriggerFallback,
  markFallbackTriggered,
  markFallbackUsed,
  getSttFailureState,

  recordBeatResult,
};

// Browser global
if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
  window.Lang003 = Lang003;
}

// CommonJS / Node
if (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) {
  module.exports = Lang003;
}
<<<END>>>

<<<FILE: scenes/ja/metro_station.js>>>
/**
 * Metro Station scene (lang_007)
 *
 * A short exchange at a Tokyo metro ticket window. The learner is
 * asking for directions and a ticket to Shinjuku. Each beat includes
 * a `fallbackText` field so the host app can show a written prompt
 * when STT fails twice in a row on the same beat.
 *
 * The scene does not change existing architecture decisions:
 *   - prompts may use hiragana + kanji
 *   - `answer` stores the target utterance in unicode (hiragana/katakana/kanji)
 *   - STT is expected to accept romaji or hiragana input
 *   - `fallbackText` is a plain English instruction shown to the learner
 */

module.exports = {
  id: 'metro_station',
  title: 'Metro Station — 駅で',
  tier: 'BEGINNER',
  beats: [
    {
      id: 'metro_b1',
      type: 'production',
      prompt: 'すみません、新宿行きの電車はどちらですか？',
      answer: 'すみません、しんじゅくゆきのでんしゃはどちらですか',
      maxStars: 3,
      allowScaffold: true,
      fallbackText: "Please say 'Excuse me, which platform is the train to Shinjuku?' in Japanese.",
    },
    {
      id: 'metro_b2',
      type: 'production',
      prompt: '新宿までの切符を一枚ください。',
      answer: 'しんじゅくまでのきっぷをいちまいいただきます',
      maxStars: 3,
      allowScaffold: true,
      fallbackText: "Please say 'One ticket to Shinjuku, please.' in Japanese.",
    },
    {
      id: 'metro_b3',
      type: 'production',
      prompt: '料金はいくらですか？',
      answer: 'りょうきんはいくらですか',
      maxStars: 3,
      allowScaffold: true,
      fallbackText: "Please say 'How much is the fare?' in Japanese.",
    },
    {
      id: 'metro_b4',
      type: 'recognition',
      prompt: 'Listen: the staff says which platform number?',
      answer: ['一', '二', '三', '四', '五', '六', '七', '八', '九', '十'],
      maxStars: 3,
      allowScaffold: true,
      fallbackText: "Please type the platform number you hear (1–10) in Japanese.",
    },
    {
      id: 'metro_b5',
      type: 'production',
      prompt: 'ありがとうございました。',
      answer: 'ありがとうございました',
      maxStars: 3,
      allowScaffold: true,
      fallbackText: "Please say 'Thank you very much.' in Japanese.",
    },
  ],
};
<<<END>>>

};

/**

 * includes a `fallbackText` field so the host app can surface a written

 * The scene follows the existing architecture:
 *   - prompts may use hiragana + kanji
 *   - `answer` stores the target utterance in unicode
 *   - STT is expected to accept romaji or hiragana input
 *   - `fallbackText` is a plain English instruction shown to the learner
 */

module.exports = {
  id: 'taxi_uber',
  title: 'Taxi / Uber — タクシーで',
  tier: 'LEARNING',
  beats: [
    {
      id: 'taxi_b1',

};

 *
 * The scene follows the existing architecture:

 *   - `answer` stores the target utterance in unicode
 *   - STT is expected to accept romaji or hiragana input
 *   - `fallbackText` is a plain English instruction shown to the learner
 */

module.exports = {

    {
      id: 'hotel_b1',

      allowScaffold: true,
      fallbackText: "Please say 'Hello. I would like to check in, please.' in Japanese.",
    },
    {

      type: 'production',
      prompt: '予約は田中です。',
      answer: 'よやくはたなかです',
      maxStars: 3,

      fallbackText: "Please say 'The reservation is under Tanaka.' in Japanese.",
    },
    {
      id: 'hotel_b3',
      type: 'production',

};

<<<FILE: scenes/ja/index.js>>>
/**
 * Registry of Japanese scenes for lang_003.
 *
 * Scenes added in lang_007 (metro_station, market_stall, taxi_uber,
 * hotel_checkin) carry a `fallbackText` field on every STT-driven beat
 * so the host app can surface a text-input prompt after 2 consecutive
 * STT failures on the same beat. Adding scenes here is the only way
 * the session orchestration glue (_session.js) can load them.
 *
 * Each scene exposes:
 *   id           {string}   unique id
 *   title        {string}   human-readable title
 *   tier         {string}   scene's intended tier (BEGINNER|LEARNING|MASTERED)
 *   beats        {Array}    ordered list of beat objects
 *     beat.id              {string}
 *     beat.type            {string}   recognition | production | listening
 *     beat.prompt          {string}
 *     beat.answer          {string|Array<string>}
 *     beat.maxStars        {number}   default 3
 *     beat.allowScaffold   {boolean}  default true; ignored when locked
 *     beat.fallbackText    {string}   (lang_007) English prompt shown when
 *                                              STT fallback is triggered
 */

const scene_001 = require('./scene_001');
const scene_002 = require('./scene_002');
const scene_003 = require('./scene_003');
const metro_station = require('./metro_station');
const market_stall = require('./market_stall');
const taxi_uber = require('./taxi_uber');
const hotel_checkin = require('./hotel_checkin');

const SCENES = Object.freeze([
  scene_001,
  scene_002,
  scene_003,
  metro_station,
  market_stall,
  taxi_uber,
  hotel_checkin,
]);

function getSceneById(id) {
  return SCENES.find((s) => s.id === id) || null;
}

function getAllBeats(scene) {
  if (!scene || !Array.isArray(scene.beats)) return [];
  return scene.beats;
}

function getTotalBeatsInScene(scene) {
  return getAllBeats(scene).length;
}

module.exports = {
  SCENES,
  getSceneById,
  getAllBeats,
  getTotalBeatsInScene,
};
<<<END>>>
